text: The planning mind is so rampant.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But really, it's the ultimate form of cloning.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Now it's just replicating the eye in the future.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: We replicate the sense of ourselves endlessly.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But somehow there's no ethics board in our minds, which is saying this isn't a good idea.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Seeing plans or thoughts in the moment is not a problem.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Planning is a fine thing to do when it's necessary to do.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But when we get caught up in it, when we get lost in it,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: then it simply strengthens this sense, this process of becoming.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: We can see the thirst for existence is craving for continued existence in the form of expectations.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The desire for something else to be happening.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's very helpful and valuable to pay careful attention to the arising of expectation.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because when we don't, it simply brings about a lot of agitation and disappointment in the mind.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It locks us into the cycle of hope and fear.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Hope that something will happen to fear that a one-happened expectation takes us out of being in the moment.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it is a setup for suffering.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: On my last retreat here, set for a month and February.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Now, I came in the last day of the retreat, the last morning to the early morning sitting where we do the metacent at the end.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And during that whole month, I was coming into that sitting and had really gotten into the beauty of doing the metacent.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we would be doing it at that time, just as a son was coming up out of the east, coming up out of the east windows.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: There's such an exquisite moment.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is the last day of the month retreat.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I come into that sitting and I had a wonderful set.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It was like the deepest set of the whole month.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It just felt like my heart opened to a whole different place of understanding.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Of course, you never know where we go out of retreat on a higher or lower, could it sit in a difficult setting.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But this time it was just this wonderful setting.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I had the thought, this is great.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I'll just end it with this metacent and I'll be the perfect end to this perfect setting.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So I'm just there, kind of, in this wonderful state.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the sun's coming up and we start the metacent.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And somebody starts joining in the chant completely out of rhythm.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the only thought that was gone through how can they ruin my perfect setting?

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This was the final culminating moment of this fall retreat.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: May all beings be happy, be peace, well, how can they ruin my setting?

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I was so striking and also so humorous.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It illuminated for me very clearly the difference between expectation and aspiration.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It illuminated the expectation that I hadn't even realized was there.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: You know, how I wanted the sitting to be, how I wanted the sitting to end, how I wanted the retreat to end.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And of course, when it didn't quite work out that way it created at least momentarily that that adjuditation.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But expectation and aspiration are two very different things.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because we can practice opening to each moment of this unfolding process without leaning into the next moment.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Without expectation.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So, in the moment without expectation, yet still be holding it with a powerful aspiration for the goal, the goal of freedom,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: the goal of not holding on, goal of letting go.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And right in this, there's an interesting merging of two models or descriptions of practice.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: One model is that, which many of you have familiar with, you know, of working through progressively the stages of insight,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: for our aggressive insight model, right up to the final goal of Nibana.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the other model of practice is that of understanding that the nature of the mind is already pure and complete.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And that we simply need to abide in this empty open awareness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Now, what I found through practicing with each of these different models, are each of these different descriptions of the practice.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: One is this progressive development through stages.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: One is just abiding in the naturally open and pure state of awareness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: What I found is that the stage model ends up simply resting in a way that we can see.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we can simply rest in awareness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: That's what happens as we progress through the stages.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And in the model of pure awareness, what I found was that the mind body simply resting in the awareness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The mind body goes through all the experiences of the stages of insight.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And so rather than see them as two different things, there's simply different ways of describing the same process.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we can use each of these descriptions, each of these different models, as a skillful means.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And in some ways, as correctives for each other, because if we're caught up in striving and expectation, if we notice that in our practice, a very useful mantra to employ at that time.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And one which has helped me a lot in my practice, the mantra is, it's already here, or already aware.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's not something to get, it's not something to look for, it's not something I need to expect or want.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's already here. The mind is already aware.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And just in that moment of reminding myself of that, can feel the letting go, can feel the relaxing back into the moment.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's the ease of non-clinging.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: On the other hand, if we're just cruising along, more or less, abiding in awareness, but really without precision, and maybe with questionable continuity,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: then periods of active mental noting can reconnect us with the unfolding process of the stages of insight.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And so the two really support and serve one another.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And both of these approaches are really skillful means for accomplishing the Buddhist advice when he said, not reviving the past, not hoping to be in the future.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Instead, with insight, see each arising state, not craving after past experience,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: not setting one's heart on future ones, not downed up with desire and craving.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So we use all the different models of practice, all the different descriptions of practice, just for this end.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's such simple advice, not reviving the past, not hoping to be in the future.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: See each arising state, not craving after past experience, not setting one's heart on future ones, even meditative ones, not bound up with desire and craving.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The mind free of craving, free of this thirst for existence, thirst for becoming.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Free of even the desire for the next moment. This engages the gears of attachment, the gears of clinging, of wanting to become anything.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Buddha Dasa, who was one of the great time masters of the last century, he summed this up beautifully and it became a useful mantra for me in my practice.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He said, nothing to do, nothing to be, nothing to have, nothing to do, nothing to be, nothing to have.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: You can experience this mind of no craving as a great relief from this thirst for becoming that really has been driving us throughout some sorrow.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's this thirst which has kept us going and which keeps us going, thirst for becoming, for wanting to be.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I want to read something, it's a little passage by Paul Bowles, the writer who spent so much time in North Africa.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And there's just his description of the experience of Sahara.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Immediately when you arrive in the Sahara for the first time, or the tenth time, you notice the stillness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Then there is the sky compared to which all other skies seem faint-hearted efforts.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Solid and luminous, it is always the focal point of the landscape.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Presently, you will either shiver and hurry back inside for mid-year walls, where you will go on standing there and let something very peculiar happen to you.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Something that everyone who lives there has undergone, and what the French call the baptism of solitude.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It is a unique sensation, and it has nothing to do with loneliness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: For loneliness presupposes memory, here in this holy mineral landscape, lighted by stars like flares, even memory disappears.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Nothing is left, but you're on breathing and the sound of your heart beating.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So in some way, I think we experience the inner Sahara where we come back to that elemental nature of what's happening.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So there's dependency in this world through the craving for sense pleasures, through the thirst of craving for continued existence.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The dependency is also created by the third kind of craving, which is the thirst for non-existence.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Somehow the sense that things are so bad, if only I could not be.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Maybe the experience this craving for non-existence to some extent, you know, in some difficult settings.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: If only it could not be, I could not be, or in difficult life situations.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Who's Chakwin, who is an Australian travel writer?

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: You wrote this wonderful book about all the awful trips he took of just kind of the worst places he ended up in the most uncomfortable places.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the title of the book which really appealed to me was, what am I doing here?

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we can see that question, or I so often both in our meditation and in our lives.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: What am I doing here?

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: In the course, the 1984 course with Saita Upandita when he first came to IMS and this very intense difficult course.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Both Michelle McDonald and I, there was a very difficult retreat and painful and a lot of, a lot of intense things.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: A lot of intensity. And afterwards we formed what we called the Duca Club.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And a motto for this Duca Club was from the poet, I think he's a central American, a not sure poet Dario.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He has a line from one of his poems, O to be a stone with no feeling at all.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Because that was the sense of this Duca Club. It's just too bad. How can we not be here?

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But the problem with this and here's where the subtlety of this kind of craving comes in.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Is that this kind of thirst for non-existence nourishes and is nourished by the view of self that there's someone there not to be around.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But there's a someone not to be reborn. But there's a someone who dies.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: It's all sustaining and nourishing this view of self.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So a particular care is needed, not only in the kind of ordinary times of desire for non-existence,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: but it's particularly important to understand this in those meditative states.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And often they come as the practice deepens where we feel intensely the Duca of conditioned existence.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I mean this is part of our growing insight, where there's a strong desire for liberation because we are feeling the Duca.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So immediately is one stage of insight called the urge for deliverance.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So at that time, that's time when I practice when we're feeling this so intensely.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: We need to be mindful of this desire and urge as another arising experience, even as it's doing its work of freeing us from attachment.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So it's a powerful experience to have and yet we don't want to identify with it and create the sense of self as if there's a self that will be liberated.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is the most subtle kind of thirst for non-existence.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The great discovery in practice is that on one level, birth and death, existence and non-existence, self and other are the great defining themes of our lives.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: On an another level, we can see that all of it is just a display of empty appearances.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Nothing is really going on at all. So this points to the other aspect, the second aspect of abiding independently, not clinging to anything in the world.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: We've talked about not being dependent through craving through the three types of craving, the sense pleasures for continued existence for non-existence.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The second aspect is not being dependent through views.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And polydward is Diti, which is most fundamentally the view of self.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: In our normal mode of perception, usually when we see or hear or smell or taste something, feel something in the body or cognizant things through the mind,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: there immediately arises this felt sense of eye of mind. I'm seeing, I'm hearing, I'm feeling, I'm thinking.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And then we further elaborate, I meditating.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Then we further elaborate, I'm a good meditator, I'm a bad meditator.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we further elaborate, I'm a good person, I'm a bad person.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And we create this whole superstructure of self on top of momentary changing conditions.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is dependent through view, where we are identifying with what's arising and creating this felt sense of eye.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: In one short and powerfully liberating teaching, the Buddha pointed out the way to freedom from this deep pendants through the views of self.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: What it has to do with the teaching happened when this man came to him, he was an aesthetic from another part of India.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He had been shipwrecked, he was a merchant actually, who had been shipwrecked on the shore of India, he was in the southwest.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And all his possessions were gone. And so he just started wearing clothes of bark, right, to cover himself.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And people saw this man just covered in bark and because aesthetics at that time in India were highly venerated.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The people came and they saw this guy just covered with bark and they thought that he was this powerful aesthetic.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And so they just started worshiping him, and venerating him, and bringing all kinds of gifts.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And after while he started to maybe wonder, maybe I am really in our hunt, you know, big people were treating him like one.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So it said that he started wandering, you know, am I?

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it said in Daiva, we had been in a formal life a relative of his.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But Daiva, who was living some nearby grove of trees, came to him and said, sorry, you're not really in our hunt.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But there is one, you know, and it's actually a cross India, and referring to the Buddha, there isn't fully awake and being.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I suggest you go, you go see him.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So this man he was by this time quite sincere in his aspiration for freedom.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: I don't know whether he went across India in his bark or he got some other clothing, but he ended up making this journey.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And meeting the Buddha as the Buddha was actually going on his arms around for food.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the man's name was Bahia, and Bahia said, you know, please teach me, I really want to be free.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And the Buddha said, well, wait, it's just some standing in the middle of the road, let's go back to the monastery when I finished my rounds and I'll teach you.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Bahia was very insistent.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And now some teach me now something may happen to you to me, so a second time, a third time.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So finally the Buddha relented after the proverbial three requests.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But the teaching had to be very succinct because they were standing just in the middle of the street.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So you gave this teaching the first part of which is very familiar.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The second part is not so familiar because it's quite a convoluted poly construction, but as we deconstruct the meaning of it, it points to this place of real freedom of mind.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So I want to read to two translations.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The first will be the convoluted one, and the second will be one that begins to make some sense of it.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So the first part of the teaching, the familiar part, the Buddha said to Bahia, when in the scene, will be only what is seen.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And in the herd, only what is heard.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When in the sensed, only what is sensed, and when in the known, only what is known.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So this is the difficult translation.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: You will not be by that.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When you are not by that, you will not be there in.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When you are not there in, you will be neither here nor there or in between.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is the end of Duka.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So most of us in English might be hard to find the end of Duka in that.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: But Vimilo, who was a poly scholar, kind of translated in a way that begins to make sense of it.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: He said, can we be in the first part when in the scene, will be only what is seen in the herd, only what is heard, in the sensed, only what is sensed, in the known, only what is known.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Then you will not be influenced by that.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Meaning you will not be carried away by craving.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: If you are not influenced by it, you are not bound to it.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: That is not creating a sense of self.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When you are not bound to it, you, meaning the self, will need to be here, which means in the sense basis.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The self will not be in the sense basis.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: No there in the sense objects, nor in between, which is consciousness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is the end of Duka.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is a powerful deconstruction of self and how to accomplish it.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When in the scene, just the scene, in the herd, just the herd, in the sense, just the sensed, in the cognized, through the mind, just the cognized,

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: then you will not be influenced by what arises.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Not carried away by craving.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: If you are not influenced, not carried away, you are not bound to it.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Bound to it by view of self.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: We don't create the sense of eye.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: When you are not bound to it, then we will need to be here, creating the eye in the sense basis, not there, not creating the eye in the sense objects.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: No, in between, not creating the eye in consciousness.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is the end of Duka.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So with this quality of bare knowing of whatever is seen, heard, felt or cognized, we are not evaluating, we are not proliferating around these basic sense impressions.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And when we practice in this way, we live as the Buddha instructed in the Sutta, we live abiding independent, not clinging to anything in the world.

source: Joseph Goldstein's Satipatthana Sutta Series Pt. 6_ The Art of Letting__watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: This is the end of suffering.
